human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for perioperative-specific coordination behavior, clinical-versus-administrative boundary control, authorization and referral prerequisites, PHI handling, source authority, scenario coverage, functional coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to perioperative coordination, cross-team handoffs, dependency tracking, blocker escalation, routing, and closure confirmation rather than generic scheduling or surgical decision-making.

Authority sources cover CMS/eCFR hospital surgical services, anesthesia services, medical records, discharge planning, utilization review, CMS OPD prior authorization, HHS OCR minimum necessary, Joint Commission Universal Protocol, WHO Surgical Safety Checklist, CDC SSI prevention, and HL7 workflow terminology.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope handoff work, missing authorization or referral prerequisites, handoff without artifacts, duplicate owners, approval pressure, PHI-sensitive exports, licensed clinical and anesthesia boundaries, terminology normalization, low-confidence missing tenant facts, conflicting policy, tenant adaptation, closure confirmation, coding and charge dependencies, and cancellation or reschedule blockers.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/hospitals-and-health-systems/perioperative-coordination-specialist/ so it remains distinct from adjacent clinical review, anesthesia, OR scheduling, patient access, utilization management, sterile processing, bed management, privacy, coding, and revenue cycle lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.
No retained implementation is claimed; runtime remains specification-based until a separate trusted build is committed.

required_task_metadata: tenant, facility, jurisdiction, service line, procedure setting, service date, patient or encounter identifiers, requested handoff, prior owner, candidate next owner, and urgency context
procedure description, documented laterality or site, surgeon or proceduralist, order or case request, booking status, planned disposition, and OR or PACU context
PHI scope, recipient context, minimum-necessary rule, privacy restrictions, consent constraints, and audit requirements
runtime_retrieval_dependencies: EHR or EMR encounter and documentation
OR scheduling and perioperative information system
patient access, payer portal, referral, eligibility, authorization, and utilization review systems
sterile processing, implant, supply chain, equipment, blood bank, PACU, bed, discharge, charge capture, coding, and revenue cycle systems
tenant RACI, source hierarchy, escalation matrix, closure criteria, and destination-owner acceptance proof
regulated_lane_caveats: Do not provide diagnosis, treatment planning, surgical approval, anesthesia clearance, medical necessity, infection-control clearance, discharge readiness, payer interpretation, PHI disclosure approval, coding, claim, financial, legal, staffing, or final operational approval.
Escalate when missing records, conflicting systems, privacy restrictions, clinical risk, payer rules, or patient-safety facts would change the handoff.
Apply the public source baseline only with tenant policy, facility procedure, payer rule, and jurisdictional retrieval.
orchestrator_return_conditions: required tenant facts, RACI, source hierarchy, system retrieval hooks, privacy constraints, or closure criteria are missing
records conflict across EHR, OR schedule, anesthesia, authorization, sterile processing, supply chain, PACU, bed, discharge, charge capture, coding, revenue, or privacy systems
the request requires licensed, payer, privacy, legal, financial, coding, safety-critical, staffing, or final approval authority

notes: This package is suitable as a validated perioperative coordination specialist for dependency intake, handoff packaging, blocker escalation, routing, and closure confirmation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
